descriptionEn: Wars between Catholics and Huguenots over their differing faiths. Although intermittent, they lasted for more than three decades.
articleEn:
As Protestant churches continued to organize and pastors continued to return from Geneva Academy, the religious background of France began to drastically change.

Between 1550 and 1565, about 1,200 Protestant churches had been formed, which served approximately two million believers. These Protestants, known as Huguenots, were a significant threat to Catholicism in France. Violent outbreaks began to occur in 1562; when the Duke of Guise, a Catholic, was assassinated in March 1563, religious peace was shattered. War between the Catholics and Huguenots continued for more than 30 years in intermittent fashion.

images.caption: Assassination of the Duke of Guise, Artist: Unknown
The Duke of Guise during the day of the barricades, Paul Lehugeur
